title: Volume-sweep microstructure
question: When several chip stocks light up with huge volume at once, does that predict the next day, and is it bigger in small caps?
finding: Clean null at every size. I split a 39-name semiconductor universe into four market-cap buckets (Small <$10B / Mid $10-100B / Large $100-500B / Mega >$500B); no sweep type beats its own basket's next-day drift once I count honestly, correct for multiplicity, benchmark vs random entry, and split out-of-sample. The 'bigger effect in smaller names' hunch shows up only as a point estimate in the smallest bucket's MIXED cell (+1.40%) and it's five wild days: insignificant against drift (p=0.31), blowing up with the threshold and out-of-sample. Size scales the noise, not the signal.
